# moodle-cli 0.4.2

## Summary

This patch release fixes Moodle deployments that use suffixed session cookie names such as `MoodleSessionmoodle`.

Some sites do not authenticate with the plain `MoodleSession` cookie name. The CLI now preserves the real cookie name from browser cookies and `okta-auth`, carries it through validation, and initializes the Moodle client with that exact cookie name.

Thanks @simon-ami for the original report and patch direction in #3. The final fix landed in #4 after being reapplied on top of the current authentication flow.

## Fixes

- Preserved suffixed Moodle session cookie names during browser cookie extraction
- Preserved suffixed Moodle session cookie names from `okta-auth` candidates
- Reused the actual cookie name during session validation and client initialization
- Kept same-value cookies under different Moodle session names as separate auth candidates

## Validation

Validated with:

- `uv run pytest -q`
